subject: How IBM Research could hit p95 < 200ms on LLM serving
body:
Hi Sriram,

A vLLM user we work with went from 800ms p95 to under 200ms — while reducing their GPU footprint by 30% during peak loads.

We did it with P95.AI — an inference optimization layer that handles batching, kernel routing, and scheduling beneath your serving stack. It's stack-agnostic and deploys in under a day.

Given IBM Research's footprint in production AI, I thought this might be relevant. Would you be open to a 15-minute demo to see the numbers for your setup?

name: Shivaram Venkataraman
title: VP Engineering
hypothesis: Pain-led: Surfaces a specific cost/latency problem first to create urgency
subject: LLM serving costs at Databricks — quick question
body:
Hi Shivaram,

Distributed Ray clusters are powerful, but when LLM serving gets uneven, GPU idle time quietly compounds into real cost.

P95.AI is an inference optimization layer that sits between your serving stack and your GPUs — it handles batching, scheduling, and kernel-level routing to hit p95 latency targets while cutting GPU spend, typically 30–45%.

No model changes. No re-architecture. Plugs into your existing setup in under a day.

Worth a 15-minute look? Happy to show you exactly what the numbers look like for your current setup.
